summary.brief: Jacqueline Adams v. Finis Fields. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: Jacqueline Adams was driving on an on-ramp entering Interstate 240. Finis Fields was driving immediately behind Ms. Adams.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Adams claimed she was stopped on a single-lane ramp, waiting for traffic to clear, when she was struck from behind by Mr. Fields.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr. Fields testified that Ms. Adams 'stopped unexpectedly' for 'no reason' on a two-lane ramp even though there were no vehicles blocking her path.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Following the accident, Ms. Adams was transported by ambulance to Baptist Memorial Hospital East, complaining of back, neck, and arm pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Thomas testified that the initial medical treatments were reasonable and made necessary by the accident.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Errol Thomas

evidence_index.text: Dr. Hoit testified that based on the information Ms. Adams provided him, the treatment she received at the Semmes-Murphy Clinic, including physical therapy, injections, and subsequent back surgery, was reasonable, necessary, and causally related to the March 2017 collision.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Daniel Hoit

evidence_index.text: Ms. Adams admitted to a significant back injury in 2004, which had required nerve blocks and kept her out of work for nearly seven months.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Hoit testified that his causation opinion assumed that Ms. Adams did not have any pain before the accident.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Daniel Hoit

evidence_index.text: Dr. Thomas noted that, absent this condition, a typical recovery should have lasted only six to twelve weeks.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Errol Thomas

evidence_index.text: The jury returned a verdict finding both parties at fault. It attributed 52% of the fault to Mr. Fields and 48% of the fault to Ms. Adams.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court denied the Motion for a New Trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court noted that the jury’s medical expense award of $8,063.00 was 'so close' to the $8,263.00 in bills for the initial treatment that it reflected 'deliberate action' and careful consideration by the jury, not a compromise.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The 52% to 48% does not on its own support the 'strong suspicion of compromise' that Plaintiff argues.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Hoit testified: 'Right. More likely than not.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Daniel Hoit

evidence_index.text: Dr. Thomas also testified that based on Ms. Adams’s MRI, she 'definitely had a degenerative condition' before the accident.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Errol Thomas

evidence_index.text: The jury appears to have concluded that the later treatment was necessitated by Ms. Adams’s undisclosed, preexisting degenerative condition rather than the 2017 accident.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We affirm the judgment of the Circuit Court for Shelby County.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.chief_complaint: Back, neck, and arm pain following a vehicle collision
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was involved in a vehicle collision on an on-ramp entering Interstate 240. She was struck from behind by another vehicle.
past_medical_history: Significant back injury in 2004 requiring nerve blocks

focused_exam.back: Pain reported, specific findings not detailed in judgment

requestables.name: MRI of the spine
requestables.description: Magnetic Resonance Imaging to assess spinal condition

reveal.result_summary: Showed degenerative condition, minimal changes from prior MRI
reveal.decision_impact: Informs treatment plan and causation analysis

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to hospital following vehicle collision

timeline_phases.name: Initial Workup and Treatment
timeline_phases.description: Initial medical treatments and imaging performed

decision_points.prompt: What should be considered in the causation analysis?

options.description: Consider pre-existing conditions and obtain prior medical records
Rely solely on patient's current report of symptoms
options.clinical_reasoning: May lead to incorrect attribution of causation

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant relied on patient's current report without prior records
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant assumed no prior pain based on patient report

expected_action_court.description: Consideration of pre-existing conditions was necessary
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated need for comprehensive history

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to consider pre-existing conditions led to incorrect causation analysis
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Comprehensive history should have been obtained

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent back surgery over 18 months after collision

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to consider pre-existing conditions
Causation analysis was flawed due to lack of comprehensive history

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Comprehensive history would have informed accurate causation analysis
